title: Pillowy Peppery Argentinian Buckwheat Flour Empanadas
description: A show-off Argentinian dish featuring buckwheat flour, kombu-cold-extract, and halal-friendly ingredients, perfect for a dinner party.
instructions: Combine buckwheat flour, salt, black pepper, and kombu powder in a bowl.
Add halal beef suet, onion, garlic, and cilantro to the bowl and mix well.
Add lime juice and egg to the mixture and knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a tablespoon of filling in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and cook the empanadas for 4-5 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Serve the empanadas hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
